description: Stage rules for the Plures AI Ecosystem Explorer. Stages: Nascent < Emerging < Established, per dimension and per track, assigned as a staged capability maturity model (see framework). Talent and policy capability tests were calibrated on the first real snapshot (2026-07) and are FROZEN with the justifications below until a versioned methodology revision. Attention rules remain provisional pending sufficient A1 data.

framework.summary: The grading is grounded in three published frameworks. (1) Stage semantics: the three grades are stages in the sense of the Capability Maturity Model (Paulk et al. 1993) - a stage is reached when its defining capabilities are all present, not when a composite score crosses a line; there is no weighted index anywhere in the pipeline. (2) Dimension structure: the three dimensions operationalize Kingdon's multiple-streams model of policy windows - Attention observes the problem stream (issue salience), Talent the policy stream (the community of specialists who can produce workable proposals), Policy the politics stream (government receptivity and institutional commitment). A policy window can only be used where all three streams are mature, so the least mature dimension is the binding constraint and the suggested entry point. (3) Capacity reading: the dimensions map onto the three policy capacities of Wu, Ramesh & Howlett (2015) - analytical capacity (Talent), political capacity (Attention/legitimacy), operational capacity (Policy/institutions).

stage_semantics.Nascent: Initial (CMM level-1 analogue): the dimension's defining capabilities are absent.
stage_semantics.Emerging: Forming (level-2 analogue): some defining capabilities are present, but not all.
stage_semantics.Established: Consolidated (level-3 analogue): every defining capability is present and comparable to the stronger G20 countries.
stage_semantics.Collecting: Not a stage: insufficient evidence. Displayed as collecting, never scored as zero, never imputed.

references: Kingdon, J.W. (1984; updated ed. 2011). Agendas, Alternatives, and Public Policies. Little, Brown / Longman.
Paulk, M.C., Curtis, B., Chrissis, M.B., & Weber, C.V. (1993). Capability Maturity Model, Version 1.1. IEEE Software 10(4), 18-27.
Wu, X., Ramesh, M., & Howlett, M. (2015). Policy capacity: A conceptual framework for understanding policy competences and capabilities. Policy and Society 34(3-4), 165-171.

entry_point.comment: Formal derivation of the suggested entry point, applied mechanically by build.py per track. Replaces the earlier ad-hoc 'lowest tier, alphabetical on ties' rule. Rationale: in Kingdon's terms the least mature stream is where coupling fails when a window opens; naming it requires it to be unique and evidenced.
rules: R1 (completeness): if any dimension on the track is Collecting, the profile is provisional. A least-mature dimension may still be reported, but it is labelled provisional and no final binding constraint is published.
R2 (consolidated): if every observed dimension is Established, the profile is consolidated - no binding constraint exists and none is invented.
R3 (uniqueness): a single entry point is named only when exactly one dimension sits at the lowest observed stage.
R4 (ties): if two or more dimensions tie at the lowest stage, the profile is balanced at that stage; all tied dimensions are reported and no ranking among them is invented.

mainstream.comment: t1_share = AI works / total works, rolling 3y. Single defining capability: AI is at least as large a research priority as in the reference G20 country. Compared against a FROZEN reference share rather than the live median, so stages cannot churn month-to-month from denominator drift.
mainstream.reference_justification: G20 median share in snapshot 2026-07 (DE=0.02033), nearly identical to the US share (0.02051), making the threshold readable as 'AI occupies at least as large a slice of national research output as it does in the median G20 country / the US'.
mainstream.nascent_justification: 0.6x reference (=1.22%) sits in a visible gap in the 2026-07 distribution between ZA (0.96%) and MX (1.37%), separating the low cluster (AR, BR, ZA, JP*) from the middle.
mainstream.known_artifact: JP share (0.15%) is implausible: Japan's total-works denominator is inflated by J-STAGE bulk indexing. JP's mainstream talent stage is unreliable until the collector filters type:article on both numerator and denominator (queued fix). Similar inflation affects ID in the opposite direction (local-journal AI volume).

frontier.comment: Staged capability model. t2 = frontier works since 2022; t3 = curated orgs + university groups. Established requires ALL capabilities (research base, organized community, scale); Nascent means NEITHER foundational capability (research base, organized community) is present; anything in between is Emerging. Reproduces the 2026-07 calibration exactly while making each stage a statement about capabilities present.

research_base.justification: At least 20 frontier works since 2022 - the cut sits in the distribution gap between ZA (19) and RU (28) in the 2026-07 snapshot, separating incidental papers from a working research base.

organized_community.justification: At least two verified-active orgs/university groups - a community, not a single outpost. AR (1 paper, 3 groups) correctly escapes Nascent: field-building exists there ahead of research.

scale.justification: t2>=100 captures the top quartile of the 2026-07 G20 distribution (US 1498, GB 403, CA 224, DE 159, CN 151, IN 116 above; FR 84 below). t3>=5 = a field, not an outpost. CN (151 works, 3 entities) stays Emerging by design - research without civil society is the KR/CN gap the explorer exists to surface.

attention.comment: Within-country only (GDELT timelinevol is % of the country's monitored coverage). ratio = mean of last 12 monthly values / mean of prior 12. If A1 data is absent the stage is null with insufficient_data=true - missing is never zero. PROVISIONAL: first collection (2026-07) returned 13/44 query sets before the time budget, and most frontier-track non-English queries returned empty timelines - thresholds below are uncalibrated guesses.

mainstream.comment: Definitional map of the curated P1 activity_level coding (low/medium/high), which already synthesizes OECD initiative count, binding instruments, dedicated bodies, and 2025-26 recency.

frontier.comment: From curated P2 checklist score (0-5). 0-1 = at most a generic strategy (Nascent: AR, RU); 2-3 = engaged but not committed (Emerging: ID, MX, SA, TR, ZA); 4-5 = multiple concrete frontier commitments (Established: the rest). Cut points match the natural clustering of the 2026-07 coding.
